tool.name: fuzz_request_parameter
tool.description:
Replay a captured request many times, swapping one parameter with each payload, score results against baseline.
Faster exploitation loop than manually calling repeat_request in a loop. Takes a captured request_id, a parameter name, a list of payloads, and the parameter location (query/body/header/cookie/path). Sends the baseline (unmodified) request once to establish reference status/size/timing, then replays with each payload substitution, and returns rows sorted by 'interest score'.
Interest heuristic scores: - Status code change (+30, +60 for 5xx server errors) - Body length delta > 50% (+15) or > 10% (+5) - Response time > 3x baseline AND > 2s (+25 — time-based blind SQLi signal)
Only returns rows above interest_threshold (default 10) so noise is filtered automatically. Hard cap 200 payloads per call.
After reviewing, use repeat_request or view_request on the most interesting `new_request_id` values to inspect the full response.

parameter.name: request_id
parameter.description: Caido request ID of the captured request to replay. Find via search_burp_proxy_history or list_requests.

parameter.name: parameter
parameter.description: Name of the parameter to fuzz (query name, body field, header name, cookie name, or a literal path substring for path fuzzing).

parameter.name: payloads
parameter.description: List of payload strings to substitute (max 200). Typically the output of load_payload_list or a custom set.

parameter.name: parameter_location
parameter.description: One of: query, body, header, cookie, path. Default query.

parameter.name: interest_threshold
parameter.description: Minimum interest score to include a row in results. Default 10.

returns.description: request_id, parameter, parameter_location, payloads_sent, baseline info, interesting_count, interesting rows (payload, status_code, response_size, response_time, interest_score, reasons, new_request_id), errors.

tool.name: diff_responses
tool.description:
Diff two captured HTTP responses side-by-side, stripping noise headers by default.
Best use after fuzz_request_parameter when one payload had a notable delta — this zooms in and shows exactly which lines differ. Also works for comparing authenticated vs unauthenticated responses, user-A vs user-B (IDOR), WAF-bypass variant vs baseline, etc.
Strips Date, X-Request-Id, X-Trace-Id, Server-Timing, Set-Cookie, ETag, Last-Modified headers by default — override via ignore_headers if you care about those specifically.

parameter.name: max_diff_lines
parameter.description: Cap on diff output lines. Default 100.

parameter.name: ignore_headers
parameter.description: Custom list of headers to exclude from diff. Pass [] to diff everything.

returns.description: identical flag, body_size_delta_bytes, header_diff unified format, body_diff unified format, ignored_headers.
